answer:
The Gladly MCP server is a production-ready integration that allows AI agents to access and manipulate Gladly data using the Model Context Protocol (MCP) [1]. It provides 65 pre-built actions for reading, creating, updating, and deleting data, designed to help agents manage customer service workflows and context [1].

While the protocol itself is an open standard for connecting AI to business tools [2][3], the Gladly-specific MCP server is provided by StackOne [1]. It manages authentication, tool execution, and security, and integrates with major AI clients like Claude Desktop, Cursor, and various agent frameworks [1].
